webrtc(3) Library Functions Manual webrtc(3)

NAME

webrtc - WebRTC P2P Connections


- WebRTC DataChannel transport for ACIP packets.

SYNOPSIS

Files


file ice_selected_pair.cpp
C++ implementation for retrieving selected ICE candidate pair. file ice.h
ICE (Interactive Connectivity Establishment) for WebRTC. file sdp.h
SDP (Session Description Protocol) for WebRTC audio/video negotiation.

Data Structures


struct webrtc_config_t
WebRTC configuration. struct webrtc_datachannel_callbacks_t
DataChannel callback structure.

Typedefs


typedef struct webrtc_peer_connection webrtc_peer_connection_t
typedef struct webrtc_data_channel webrtc_data_channel_t
typedef void(* webrtc_state_callback_t) (webrtc_peer_connection_t *pc, webrtc_state_t state, void *user_data)
Callback for state changes. typedef void(* webrtc_local_description_callback_t) (webrtc_peer_connection_t *pc, const char *sdp, const char *type, void *user_data)
Callback for local SDP offer/answer generation. typedef void(* webrtc_local_candidate_callback_t) (webrtc_peer_connection_t *pc, const char *candidate, const char *mid, void *user_data)
Callback for local ICE candidate discovery. typedef void(* webrtc_gathering_state_callback_t) (webrtc_peer_connection_t *pc, webrtc_gathering_state_t state, void *user_data)
Callback for ICE gathering state changes. typedef void(* webrtc_datachannel_open_callback_t) (webrtc_data_channel_t *dc, void *user_data)
Callback for DataChannel open event. typedef void(* webrtc_datachannel_message_callback_t) (webrtc_data_channel_t *dc, const uint8_t *data, size_t size, void *user_data)
Callback for DataChannel message received. typedef void(* webrtc_datachannel_error_callback_t) (webrtc_data_channel_t *dc, const char *error, void *user_data)
Callback for DataChannel error.

Enumerations


enum webrtc_state_t { WEBRTC_STATE_NEW = 0, WEBRTC_STATE_CONNECTING, WEBRTC_STATE_CONNECTED, WEBRTC_STATE_DISCONNECTED, WEBRTC_STATE_FAILED, WEBRTC_STATE_CLOSED }
WebRTC connection state. enum webrtc_gathering_state_t { WEBRTC_GATHERING_NEW = 0, WEBRTC_GATHERING_GATHERING, WEBRTC_GATHERING_COMPLETE }
ICE gathering state.

Functions


asciichat_error_t webrtc_init (void)
Initialize WebRTC library (libdatachannel). void webrtc_destroy (void)
Cleanup WebRTC library resources. asciichat_error_t webrtc_create_peer_connection (const webrtc_config_t *config, webrtc_peer_connection_t **pc_out)
Create a new WebRTC peer connection. void webrtc_close_peer_connection (webrtc_peer_connection_t *pc)
Close and destroy a peer connection. webrtc_state_t webrtc_get_state (webrtc_peer_connection_t *pc)
Get current connection state. webrtc_gathering_state_t webrtc_get_gathering_state (webrtc_peer_connection_t *pc)
Get current ICE gathering state. bool webrtc_is_gathering_timed_out (webrtc_peer_connection_t *pc, uint32_t timeout_ms)
Check if ICE gathering has timed out. void * webrtc_get_user_data (webrtc_peer_connection_t *pc)
Get user data pointer from connection. asciichat_error_t webrtc_create_offer (webrtc_peer_connection_t *pc)
Create and set local SDP offer (for connection initiator). asciichat_error_t webrtc_set_remote_description (webrtc_peer_connection_t *pc, const char *sdp, const char *type)
Set remote SDP offer/answer. asciichat_error_t webrtc_add_remote_candidate (webrtc_peer_connection_t *pc, const char *candidate, const char *mid)
Add remote ICE candidate. asciichat_error_t webrtc_create_datachannel (webrtc_peer_connection_t *pc, const char *label, webrtc_data_channel_t **dc_out)
Create a DataChannel (for connection initiator). asciichat_error_t webrtc_datachannel_send (webrtc_data_channel_t *dc, const uint8_t *data, size_t size)
Send data over DataChannel. bool webrtc_datachannel_is_open (webrtc_data_channel_t *dc)
Check if DataChannel is open and ready. void webrtc_datachannel_set_open_state (webrtc_data_channel_t *dc, bool is_open)
Set DataChannel open state (internal use). const char * webrtc_datachannel_get_label (webrtc_data_channel_t *dc)
Get DataChannel label. asciichat_error_t webrtc_datachannel_set_callbacks (webrtc_data_channel_t *dc, const webrtc_datachannel_callbacks_t *callbacks)
Set DataChannel callbacks. void webrtc_close_datachannel (webrtc_data_channel_t *dc)
Close a DataChannel. void webrtc_datachannel_destroy (webrtc_data_channel_t *dc)
Destroy a DataChannel and free resources. void webrtc_peer_connection_close (webrtc_peer_connection_t *pc)
Close a peer connection. void webrtc_peer_connection_destroy (webrtc_peer_connection_t *pc)
Destroy a peer connection and free resources. int webrtc_get_rtc_id (webrtc_peer_connection_t *pc)
Get the internal libdatachannel peer connection ID.

Detailed Description

WebRTC DataChannel transport for ACIP packets.

Typedef Documentation

typedef struct webrtc_data_channel webrtc_data_channel_t

#include <include/ascii-chat/network/webrtc/webrtc.h>

Definition at line 36 of file include/ascii-chat/network/webrtc/webrtc.h.

typedef void(* webrtc_datachannel_error_callback_t) (webrtc_data_channel_t *dc, const char *error, void *user_data)

#include <include/ascii-chat/network/webrtc/webrtc.h>

Callback for DataChannel error.

Parameters

dc Data channel
error Error message (null-terminated)
user_data User-provided context pointer

Definition at line 123 of file include/ascii-chat/network/webrtc/webrtc.h.

typedef void(* webrtc_datachannel_message_callback_t) (webrtc_data_channel_t *dc, const uint8_t *data, size_t size, void *user_data)

#include <include/ascii-chat/network/webrtc/webrtc.h>

Callback for DataChannel message received.

Parameters

dc Data channel
data Message data (binary)
size Message size in bytes
user_data User-provided context pointer

Definition at line 114 of file include/ascii-chat/network/webrtc/webrtc.h.

#include <include/ascii-chat/network/webrtc/webrtc.h>

Callback for DataChannel open event.

Parameters

dc Data channel that opened
user_data User-provided context pointer

Definition at line 105 of file include/ascii-chat/network/webrtc/webrtc.h.

typedef void(* webrtc_gathering_state_callback_t) (webrtc_peer_connection_t *pc, webrtc_gathering_state_t state, void *user_data)

#include <include/ascii-chat/network/webrtc/webrtc.h>

Callback for ICE gathering state changes.

Parameters

pc Peer connection
state New gathering state
user_data User-provided context pointer

Definition at line 97 of file include/ascii-chat/network/webrtc/webrtc.h.

typedef void(* webrtc_local_candidate_callback_t) (webrtc_peer_connection_t *pc, const char *candidate, const char *mid, void *user_data)

#include <include/ascii-chat/network/webrtc/webrtc.h>

Callback for local ICE candidate discovery.

Parameters

pc Peer connection
candidate ICE candidate string (null-terminated)
mid Media stream ID (null-terminated)
user_data User-provided context pointer

Application should send this candidate to the remote peer via signaling channel (ACDS).

Definition at line 88 of file include/ascii-chat/network/webrtc/webrtc.h.

typedef void(* webrtc_local_description_callback_t) (webrtc_peer_connection_t *pc, const char *sdp, const char *type, void *user_data)

#include <include/ascii-chat/network/webrtc/webrtc.h>

Callback for local SDP offer/answer generation.

Parameters

pc Peer connection
sdp SDP string (null-terminated)
type SDP type ("offer" or "answer")
user_data User-provided context pointer

Application should send this SDP to the remote peer via signaling channel (ACDS).

Definition at line 76 of file include/ascii-chat/network/webrtc/webrtc.h.

Parameters

pc Peer connection
state New connection state

Enumeration Type Documentation

enum webrtc_gathering_state_t

#include <include/ascii-chat/network/webrtc/webrtc.h>

ICE gathering state.

Enumerator

WEBRTC_GATHERING_NEW
Not started.
WEBRTC_GATHERING_GATHERING
Gathering candidates.
WEBRTC_GATHERING_COMPLETE
All candidates gathered.

Definition at line 53 of file include/ascii-chat/network/webrtc/webrtc.h.

53              {
54   WEBRTC_GATHERING_NEW = 0,   
55   WEBRTC_GATHERING_GATHERING, 
56   WEBRTC_GATHERING_COMPLETE   
57 } webrtc_gathering_state_t;








enum webrtc_state_t


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/network/webrtc/webrtc.h>


WebRTC connection state.


Enumerator
  





  
WEBRTC_STATE_NEW
    

  
Connection created but not started.

  
WEBRTC_STATE_CONNECTING
    

  
ICE gathering/connection in progress.

  
WEBRTC_STATE_CONNECTED
    

  
DataChannel established and ready.

  
WEBRTC_STATE_DISCONNECTED
    

  
Connection lost.

  
WEBRTC_STATE_FAILED
    

  
Connection failed (fatal).

  
WEBRTC_STATE_CLOSED
    

  
Connection closed cleanly.




Definition at line 41 of file
    include/ascii-chat/network/webrtc/webrtc.h.


41              {
42   WEBRTC_STATE_NEW = 0,      
43   WEBRTC_STATE_CONNECTING,   
44   WEBRTC_STATE_CONNECTED,    
45   WEBRTC_STATE_DISCONNECTED, 
46   WEBRTC_STATE_FAILED,       
47   WEBRTC_STATE_CLOSED        
48 } webrtc_state_t;












Function
  Documentation




asciichat_error_t webrtc_add_remote_candidate
  (webrtc_peer_connection_t * pc, const char * candidate, const char *
  mid)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/network/webrtc/webrtc.h>


Add remote ICE candidate.


Parameters


pc Peer connection


candidate ICE candidate string (null-terminated)


mid Media stream ID (null-terminated, can be empty)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Adds a remote ICE candidate received via signaling channel.


Definition at line 574 of file
    lib/network/webrtc/webrtc.c.


574                                                                                                                     {
575   if (!pc || !candidate) {
576     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters");
577   }
578 
579   log_debug("  [4] Before libdatachannel - candidate: '%s' (len=%zu)", candidate, strlen(candidate));
580   log_debug("  [4] Before libdatachannel - mid: '%s' (len=%zu)", mid ? mid : "(null)", mid ? strlen(mid) : 0);
581 
582   int result = rtcAddRemoteCandidate(pc->rtc_id, candidate, mid);
583   if (result != RTC_ERR_SUCCESS) {
584     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to add remote ICE candidate (rtc error %d)", result);
585   }
586 
587   log_debug("Added remote ICE candidate (pc_id=%d)", pc->rtc_id);
588   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
589 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_NETWORK, log_debug,
    webrtc_peer_connection::rtc_id, and SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by ice_add_remote_candidate(), and
    webrtc_peer_manager_handle_ice().






void
  webrtc_close_datachannel (webrtc_data_channel_t * dc)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/network/webrtc/webrtc.h>


Close a DataChannel.


Parameters


dc Data channel to close


Closes the channel gracefully. Safe to call with NULL pointer.


Definition at line 699 of file
    lib/network/webrtc/webrtc.c.


699                                                          {
700   if (!dc) {
701     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "DataChannel is NULL");
702     return;
703   }
704 
705   // Save the ID before freeing (dc might be partially freed already)
706   int dc_id = dc->rtc_id;
707 
708   rtcDeleteDataChannel(dc_id);
709   log_debug("Closed DataChannel (dc_id=%d)", dc_id);
710 
711   SAFE_FREE(dc);
712 }






References ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, log_debug,
    webrtc_data_channel::rtc_id, SAFE_FREE, and
  SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by webrtc_close_peer_connection(), and
    webrtc_datachannel_destroy().






void
  webrtc_close_peer_connection (webrtc_peer_connection_t * pc)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/network/webrtc/webrtc.h>


Close and destroy a peer connection.


Parameters


pc Peer connection to close


Closes the connection gracefully and frees all resources. Safe to
    call with NULL pointer.


Definition at line 467 of file
    lib/network/webrtc/webrtc.c.


467                                                                 {
468   if (!pc) {
469     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid peer connection");
470     return;
471   }
472 
473   // Close data channel if exists
474   if (pc->dc) {
475     webrtc_close_datachannel(pc->dc);
476     pc->dc = NULL;
477   }
478 
479   // Close peer connection
480   rtcDeletePeerConnection(pc->rtc_id);
481   log_debug("Closed WebRTC peer connection (id=%d)", pc->rtc_id);
482 
483   SAFE_FREE(pc);
484 }






References webrtc_peer_connection::dc,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, log_debug,
    webrtc_peer_connection::rtc_id, SAFE_FREE, SET_ERRNO,
    and webrtc_close_datachannel().






asciichat_error_t webrtc_create_datachannel
  (webrtc_peer_connection_t * pc, const char * label,
  webrtc_data_channel_t ** dc_out)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/network/webrtc/webrtc.h>


Create a DataChannel (for connection initiator).


Parameters


pc Peer connection


label Channel label (e.g., "acip")


dc_out Output parameter for data channel handle


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Creates a new DataChannel. The initiator should create the
    channel, while the responder receives it via the datachannel callback.


Definition at line 595 of file
    lib/network/webrtc/webrtc.c.


596                                                                             {
597   if (!pc || !label || !dc_out) {
598     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters");
599   }
600 
601   // Create data channel
602   int dc_id = rtcCreateDataChannel(pc->rtc_id, label);
603   if (dc_id < 0) {
604     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to create data channel (rtc error %d)", dc_id);
605   }
606 
607   // Allocate wrapper
608   webrtc_data_channel_t *dc = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(webrtc_data_channel_t), webrtc_data_channel_t *);
609   if (!dc) {
610     rtcDeleteDataChannel(dc_id);
611     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate data channel wrapper");
612   }
613 
614   dc->rtc_id = dc_id;
615   dc->pc = pc;
616   dc->is_open = false;
617 
618   // Set up callbacks
619   rtcSetUserPointer(dc_id, dc);
620   rtcSetOpenCallback(dc_id, on_datachannel_open_adapter);
621   rtcSetMessageCallback(dc_id, on_datachannel_message_adapter);
622   rtcSetErrorCallback(dc_id, on_datachannel_error_adapter);
623 
624   pc->dc = dc;
625   *dc_out = dc;
626 
627   // Register data channel with debug naming system
628   NAMED_REGISTER_DATACHANNEL(dc, label, NULL);
629 
630   log_debug("Created DataChannel '%s' (dc_id=%d, pc_id=%d)", label, dc_id, pc->rtc_id);
631   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
632 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, webrtc_peer_connection::dc,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_MEMORY, ERROR_NETWORK,
    webrtc_data_channel::is_open, log_debug,
    NAMED_REGISTER_DATACHANNEL, webrtc_data_channel::pc,
    webrtc_data_channel::rtc_id, webrtc_peer_connection::rtc_id,
    SAFE_MALLOC, and SET_ERRNO.






asciichat_error_t webrtc_create_offer
  (webrtc_peer_connection_t * pc)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/network/webrtc/webrtc.h>


Create and set local SDP offer (for connection initiator).


Parameters


pc Peer connection


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Triggers ICE gathering and local description callback with the SDP
    offer. Use this when initiating a connection to a remote peer.


Definition at line 541 of file
    lib/network/webrtc/webrtc.c.


541                                                                     {
542   if (!pc) {
543     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid peer connection");
544   }
545 
546   // Set local description with NULL type to trigger offer generation
547   int result = rtcSetLocalDescription(pc->rtc_id, NULL);
548   if (result != RTC_ERR_SUCCESS) {
549     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to create SDP offer (rtc error %d)", result);
550   }
551 
552   log_debug("Creating SDP offer (pc_id=%d)", pc->rtc_id);
553   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
554 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_NETWORK, log_debug,
    webrtc_peer_connection::rtc_id, and SET_ERRNO.






asciichat_error_t webrtc_create_peer_connection (const
  webrtc_config_t * config, webrtc_peer_connection_t **
  pc_out)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/network/webrtc/webrtc.h>


Create a new WebRTC peer connection.


Parameters


Config Module Configuration
  including ICE servers and callbacks


pc_out Output parameter for peer connection handle


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Creates a new peer connection with the specified configuration.
    The connection starts in WEBRTC_STATE_NEW state.


Definition at line 381 of file
    lib/network/webrtc/webrtc.c.


381                                                                                                                   {
382   if (!config || !pc_out) {
383     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid config or output parameter");
384   }
385 
386   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_webrtc_lc)) {
387     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "WebRTC library not initialized");
388   }
389 
390   // Allocate peer connection wrapper
391   webrtc_peer_connection_t *pc = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(webrtc_peer_connection_t), webrtc_peer_connection_t *);
392   if (!pc) {
393     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate peer connection");
394   }
395 
396   pc->config = *config; // Copy config
397   pc->state = WEBRTC_STATE_NEW;
398   pc->dc = NULL;
399 
400   // Build ICE server list for libdatachannel
401   const char **ice_servers = NULL;
402   size_t ice_count = 0;
403 
404   if (config->stun_count > 0 || config->turn_count > 0) {
405     ice_count = config->stun_count + config->turn_count;
406     ice_servers = SAFE_MALLOC(ice_count * sizeof(char *), const char **);
407     if (!ice_servers) {
408       SAFE_FREE(pc);
409       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate ICE server list");
410     }
411 
412     // Add STUN servers
413     for (size_t i = 0; i < config->stun_count; i++) {
414       ice_servers[i] = config->stun_servers[i].host;
415     }
416 
417     // Add TURN servers
418     for (size_t i = 0; i < config->turn_count; i++) {
419       ice_servers[config->stun_count + i] = config->turn_servers[i].url;
420     }
421   }
422 
423   // Create libdatachannel configuration
424   rtcConfiguration rtc_config;
425   memset(&rtc_config, 0, sizeof(rtc_config));
426   rtc_config.iceServers = ice_servers;
427   rtc_config.iceServersCount = (int)ice_count;
428   rtc_config.iceTransportPolicy = RTC_TRANSPORT_POLICY_ALL;
429 
430   // Create peer connection
431   int pc_id = rtcCreatePeerConnection(&rtc_config);
432 
433   // Free ICE server list (libdatachannel makes a copy)
434   if (ice_servers) {
435     SAFE_FREE(ice_servers);
436   }
437 
438   if (pc_id < 0) {
439     SAFE_FREE(pc);
440     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to create peer connection (rtc error %d)", pc_id);
441   }
442 
443   pc->rtc_id = pc_id;
444 
445   // Initialize gathering state
446   pc->gathering_state = WEBRTC_GATHERING_NEW;
447   pc->gathering_start_time_ms = 0;
448 
449   // Set up callbacks
450   rtcSetUserPointer(pc_id, pc);
451   rtcSetStateChangeCallback(pc_id, on_state_change_adapter);
452   rtcSetGatheringStateChangeCallback(pc_id, on_gathering_state_change_adapter);
453   rtcSetLocalDescriptionCallback(pc_id, on_local_description_adapter);
454   rtcSetLocalCandidateCallback(pc_id, on_local_candidate_adapter);
455   rtcSetDataChannelCallback(pc_id, on_datachannel_adapter);
456 
457   /* Register peer connection with named registry */
458   char pc_name[64];
459   snprintf(pc_name, sizeof(pc_name), "webrtc_peer_connection:%d", pc_id);
460   NAMED_REGISTER(pc, pc_name, "webrtc_peer_connection_t", "0x%tx", NULL);
461 
462   *pc_out = pc;
463   log_debug("Created WebRTC peer connection (id=%d)", pc_id);
464   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
465 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    webrtc_peer_connection::config, webrtc_peer_connection::dc,
    ERROR_INIT, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_MEMORY,
    ERROR_NETWORK,
    webrtc_peer_connection::gathering_start_time_ms,
    webrtc_peer_connection::gathering_state,
    lifecycle_is_initialized(), log_debug, NAMED_REGISTER,
    webrtc_peer_connection::rtc_id, SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC,
    SET_ERRNO, webrtc_peer_connection::state,
    webrtc_config_t::stun_count, webrtc_config_t::stun_servers,
    webrtc_config_t::turn_count, webrtc_config_t::turn_servers,
    WEBRTC_GATHERING_NEW, and WEBRTC_STATE_NEW.






void
  webrtc_datachannel_destroy (webrtc_data_channel_t * dc)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/network/webrtc/webrtc.h>


Destroy a DataChannel and free resources.


Parameters


dc Data channel to destroy


Frees all resources associated with the DataChannel. Automatically
    closes the channel if still open. Safe to call with NULL pointer.


Definition at line 760 of file
    lib/network/webrtc/webrtc.c.


760                                                            {
761   if (!dc) {
762     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid null data channel");
763     return;
764   }
765 
766   // Close if still open, then free
767   webrtc_close_datachannel(dc);
768   // Note: webrtc_close_datachannel already calls SAFE_FREE(dc), so we're done
769 }






References ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, SET_ERRNO, and
    webrtc_close_datachannel().






const
  char * webrtc_datachannel_get_label (webrtc_data_channel_t *
  dc)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/network/webrtc/webrtc.h>


Get DataChannel label.


Parameters


dc Data channel


Returns


Label string (null-terminated), or NULL on error


Definition at line 684 of file
    lib/network/webrtc/webrtc.c.


684                                                                     {
685   if (!dc) {
686     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "DataChannel is NULL");
687     return NULL;
688   }
689 
690   static char label[256];
691   int result = rtcGetDataChannelLabel(dc->rtc_id, label, sizeof(label));
692   if (result < 0) {
693     return NULL;
694   }
695 
696   return label;
697 }






References ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    webrtc_data_channel::rtc_id, and SET_ERRNO.






bool webrtc_datachannel_is_open
  (webrtc_data_channel_t * dc)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/network/webrtc/webrtc.h>


Check if DataChannel is open and ready.


Parameters


dc Data channel


Returns


true if open, false otherwise


Definition at line 668 of file
    lib/network/webrtc/webrtc.c.


668                                                            {
669   if (!dc) {
670     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "DataChannel is NULL");
671     return false;
672   }
673   return dc->is_open;
674 }






References ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    webrtc_data_channel::is_open, and SET_ERRNO.






asciichat_error_t webrtc_datachannel_send
  (webrtc_data_channel_t * dc, const uint8_t * data, size_t
  size)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/network/webrtc/webrtc.h>


Send data over DataChannel.


Parameters


dc Data channel


data Data buffer (binary)


size Data size in bytes


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Sends binary data over the DataChannel. Returns error if channel
    is not open.


Definition at line 634 of file
    lib/network/webrtc/webrtc.c.


634                                                                                                        {
635   if (!dc || !data) {
636     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters");
637   }
638 
639   if (!dc->is_open) {
640     log_error("★ WEBRTC_DATACHANNEL_SEND: Channel not open! size=%zu, dc->rtc_id=%d", size, dc ? dc->rtc_id : -1);
641     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "DataChannel not open");
642   }
643 
644   // Log packet details at network layer (debug level for normal operation)
645   if (size >= 20) {
646     const uint8_t *pkt = (const uint8_t *)data;
647     log_debug("★ RTCSENDMESSAGE_BEFORE: dc_id=%d, size=%zu, first_20_bytes: %02x%02x%02x%02x %02x%02x%02x%02x "
648               "%02x%02x%02x%02x %02x%02x%02x%02x %02x%02x%02x%02x",
649               dc->rtc_id, size, pkt[0], pkt[1], pkt[2], pkt[3], pkt[4], pkt[5], pkt[6], pkt[7], pkt[8], pkt[9], pkt[10],
650               pkt[11], pkt[12], pkt[13], pkt[14], pkt[15], pkt[16], pkt[17], pkt[18], pkt[19]);
651   } else {
652     log_debug("★ RTCSENDMESSAGE_BEFORE: dc_id=%d, size=%zu (too small to log content)", dc->rtc_id, size);
653   }
654 
655   int result = rtcSendMessage(dc->rtc_id, (const char *)data, (int)size);
656 
657   log_debug("★ RTCSENDMESSAGE_AFTER: dc_id=%d, rtcSendMessage returned %d for size=%zu", dc->rtc_id, result, size);
658 
659   if (result < 0) {
660     log_error("★ WEBRTC_DATACHANNEL_SEND: FAILED with error code %d", result);
661     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send data (rtc error %d)", result);
662   }
663 
664   log_debug("★ WEBRTC_DATACHANNEL_SEND: SUCCESS - sent %zu bytes", size);
665   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
666 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_NETWORK, webrtc_data_channel::is_open, log_debug,
    log_error, webrtc_data_channel::rtc_id, and
  SET_ERRNO.






asciichat_error_t webrtc_datachannel_set_callbacks
  (webrtc_data_channel_t * dc, const
  webrtc_datachannel_callbacks_t * callbacks)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/network/webrtc/webrtc.h>


Set DataChannel callbacks.


Parameters


dc Data channel


callbacks Callback structure


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Registers callbacks for DataChannel events. Any NULL callback
    pointer is ignored.


Definition at line 718 of file
    lib/network/webrtc/webrtc.c.


719                                                                                                     {
720   if (!dc) {
721     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "DataChannel is NULL");
722   }
723 
724   if (!callbacks) {
725     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Callbacks struct is NULL");
726   }
727 
728   // Store user callbacks in the data channel struct
729   dc->user_on_open = callbacks->on_open;
730   dc->user_on_close = callbacks->on_close;
731   dc->user_on_error = callbacks->on_error;
732   dc->user_on_message = callbacks->on_message;
733   dc->user_data = callbacks->user_data;
734 
735   // Register adapter functions with libdatachannel (adapters use correct signature)
736   // The adapters will look up our stored callbacks and invoke them with proper types
737   if (callbacks->on_open) {
738     rtcSetOpenCallback(dc->rtc_id, on_datachannel_open_adapter);
739   }
740 
741   if (callbacks->on_close) {
742     rtcSetClosedCallback(dc->rtc_id, on_datachannel_closed_adapter);
743   }
744 
745   if (callbacks->on_error) {
746     rtcSetErrorCallback(dc->rtc_id, on_datachannel_error_adapter);
747   }
748 
749   if (callbacks->on_message) {
750     rtcSetMessageCallback(dc->rtc_id, on_datachannel_message_adapter);
751   }
752 
753   // Set user pointer to the data channel so adapters can retrieve it
754   rtcSetUserPointer(dc->rtc_id, dc);
755 
756   log_debug("Set DataChannel callbacks (dc_id=%d)", dc->rtc_id);
757   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
758 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    log_debug, webrtc_datachannel_callbacks_t::on_close,
    webrtc_datachannel_callbacks_t::on_error,
    webrtc_datachannel_callbacks_t::on_message,
    webrtc_datachannel_callbacks_t::on_open,
    webrtc_data_channel::rtc_id, SET_ERRNO,
    webrtc_data_channel::user_data,
    webrtc_datachannel_callbacks_t::user_data,
    webrtc_data_channel::user_on_close,
    webrtc_data_channel::user_on_error,
    webrtc_data_channel::user_on_message, and
    webrtc_data_channel::user_on_open.


Referenced by acip_webrtc_transport_create().

Parameters


dc Data channel


is_open Open state to set


Internal function for transport layer to manually set open state
    when callbacks are replaced after the DataChannel is already open.

679     return;

681   dc->is_open = is_open;
682 }

void
  webrtc_destroy (void )


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/network/webrtc/webrtc.h>


Cleanup WebRTC library resources. Should be called at program exit
    after all connections are closed. Thread-safe and idempotent.

    lib/network/webrtc/webrtc.c.

374   webrtc_release();
375 }

asciichat_error_t webrtc_init (void )


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/network/webrtc/webrtc.h>


Initialize WebRTC library (libdatachannel).


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Must be called once before creating any peer connections.
    Thread-safe and idempotent (safe to call multiple times).


Definition at line 369 of file
    lib/network/webrtc/webrtc.c.


369                                     {
370   return webrtc_ensure_initialized();

Referenced by server_main().






bool webrtc_is_gathering_timed_out
  (webrtc_peer_connection_t * pc, uint32_t timeout_ms)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/network/webrtc/webrtc.h>


Check if ICE gathering has timed out.


Parameters


pc Peer connection


timeout_ms Timeout in milliseconds


Returns


true if gathering is in progress and has exceeded
  timeout, false otherwise


Definition at line 502 of file
    lib/network/webrtc/webrtc.c.


502                                                                                       {
503   if (!pc) {
504     return false;
505   }
506 
507   // Only check timeout if we're actively gathering
508   if (pc->gathering_state != WEBRTC_GATHERING_GATHERING) {
509     return false;
510   }
511 
512   // If gathering hasn't started yet (start_time == 0), no timeout
513   if (pc->gathering_start_time_ms == 0) {
514     return false;
515   }
516 
517   // Check if elapsed time exceeds timeout
518   uint64_t current_time_ms = platform_get_monotonic_time_us() / 1000;
519   uint64_t elapsed_ms = current_time_ms - pc->gathering_start_time_ms;
520 
521   if (elapsed_ms > timeout_ms) {
522     log_warn("ICE gathering timeout: %llu ms elapsed (timeout: %u ms)", (unsigned long long)elapsed_ms, timeout_ms);
523     return true;
524   }
525 
526   return false;
527 }






References webrtc_peer_connection::gathering_start_time_ms,
    webrtc_peer_connection::gathering_state, log_warn,
    platform_get_monotonic_time_us(), and
    WEBRTC_GATHERING_GATHERING.


Referenced by
    webrtc_peer_manager_check_gathering_timeouts().

  webrtc_peer_connection_close (webrtc_peer_connection_t * pc)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/network/webrtc/webrtc.h>


Close a peer connection.


Parameters


pc Peer connection to close


Closes the peer connection gracefully. Safe to call with NULL
    pointer.


Definition at line 775 of file
    lib/network/webrtc/webrtc.c.


775                                                                 {
776   if (!pc) {
777     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid null peer connectiont");
778     return;
779   }
780 
781   rtcClose(pc->rtc_id);
782   log_debug("Closed peer connection (pc_id=%d)", pc->rtc_id);
783 }






References ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, log_debug,
    webrtc_peer_connection::rtc_id, and SET_ERRNO.






void
  webrtc_peer_connection_destroy (webrtc_peer_connection_t * pc)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/network/webrtc/webrtc.h>


Destroy a peer connection and free resources.


Parameters


pc Peer connection to destroy


Frees all resources associated with the peer connection.
    Automatically closes the connection if still open. Safe to call with NULL
    pointer.


Definition at line 785 of file
    lib/network/webrtc/webrtc.c.


785                                                                   {
786   if (!pc) {
787     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid null peer connectiont");
788     return;
789   }
790 
791   // Close and delete peer connection
792   rtcDeletePeerConnection(pc->rtc_id);
793   log_debug("Destroyed peer connection (pc_id=%d)", pc->rtc_id);
794 
795   SAFE_FREE(pc);
796 }






References ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, log_debug,
    webrtc_peer_connection::rtc_id, SAFE_FREE, and
    SET_ERRNO.






asciichat_error_t webrtc_set_remote_description
  (webrtc_peer_connection_t * pc, const char * sdp, const char *
  type)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/network/webrtc/webrtc.h>


Set remote SDP offer/answer.


Parameters


pc Peer connection


sdp Remote SDP string (null-terminated)


type SDP type ("offer" or "answer")


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Sets the remote peer's SDP. If this is an offer, triggers
    automatic answer generation via the local description callback.


Definition at line 556 of file
    lib/network/webrtc/webrtc.c.


556                                                                                                                  {
557   if (!pc || !sdp || !type) {
558     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters");
559   }
560 
561   int result = rtcSetRemoteDescription(pc->rtc_id, sdp, type);
562   if (result != RTC_ERR_SUCCESS) {
563     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to set remote SDP (rtc error %d)", result);
564   }
565 
566   log_debug("Set remote SDP description (pc_id=%d, type=%s)", pc->rtc_id, type);
567   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
568 }
